---
title: 'Credit Cards'
date: '2012-10-08T07:00:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '1556205443653846962'
blogger_filename: '/2012/10/credit-cards.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Studies'
  - 'Prosperity'
  - 'Finances'
  - 'Keys To Success'
  - 'Problem Solving'
---

<h3>Credit Cards</h3><h3 style="text-align: center;"><b><span style="font-size: large;">Question</span>:</b></h3><h4 style="text-align: center;"><i>"I've heard that you have to get a credit card to build credit, but so many people build up credit card debt and I'm afraid to do it... "</i></h4><div style="text-align: center;"><i>" I was told to get a credit card and put a $300 balance on it and make payments on it for 6 months to year... I'm very concerned because I've heard so much about credit cards causing harm..."</i></div><div style="text-align: center;"><i><br /></i></div><div style="text-align: center;"><i>"I had a card when I was young and totally messed up my life with it... I don't want another one..."</i></div><div style="text-align: center;"><i><br /></i></div><div style="text-align: center;"><i>"I don't want to use a credit card, I pay cash for everything..."</i></div><div style="text-align: center;"><i><br /></i></div><div style="text-align: center;"><br /></div><h3 style="text-align: center;"><b><span style="font-size: large;">Answer</span>:</b></h3><div style="text-align: center;"><br /></div><h4><i>I want to take a moment to talk about credit cards. The Good, The Bad, The Ugly, and The Not As Ugly As You Think Because You Aren't Thinking Of Them Right... Is that too long?</i></h4><div><span style="font-size: large;"><br /></span></div><div><i>What you are about to read takes me 30 minutes to an hour to explain verbally with people sometimes, because they ask questions and I have to keep checking for understanding. It took me two hours to write. You can read it in less than 10 minutes.&nbsp;</i></div><div><i><br /></i></div><div><i>**If you read nothing else, read the end: How does a credit card affect you?**</i></div><div><i><br /></i></div><h4 style="text-align: center;">The Bad and Ugly</h4><div>Credit Card <u>Debt</u>... is evil! It's a tool of the enemy to keep you subject to fear and bondage. Satan has designed this debtors system from the ground up. Every time you vote for a person, from either party, that wants more Government Spending, you are voting for more debt. Debt is BAD. <a href="http://bible.cc/proverbs/22-7.htm" target="_blank">The Borrower becomes a servant or slave to the lender</a>. After years of working with people's finances I can assure you this is TRUE!</div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div>Is debt a sin? No. God said that he'd make you a<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/deuteronomy/passage.aspx?q=deuteronomy+28:12-13" target="_blank"> lender and not a borrower</a>. Well, you can't lend to people if it's a sin, because you'd be helping them sin and God doesn't empower you to help people sin. But it's not God's Highest will for any person to be in or stay in debt.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>A young child get's out of high school and goes to college and gets a credit card. He sees an $800 limit. He's never even made $800 a month at his job yet, but he sees this as a path to get things he wants. "I can't afford that... wait, no problem, I'll put it on the credit card..." 10 years later he's still paying that card off... Sound bad? It was me! I know first hand, credit cards can be a powerful tool of slavery if used incorrectly.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>However...</div><h4 style="text-align: center;">The Good</h4><div>Money, Credit, Hammer, Nail. What does these four have in common? They are just tools.&nbsp;Neither&nbsp;good nor bad. A hammer can be a powerful tool to smash a vase! A hammer can be a powerful tool to build a home. It's just a tool. It's the heart and understanding of the one who uses it that determines it's effectiveness and direction. Passion, without direction, is pointless. So having the right heart, but not the knowledge and skill is still without any positive effect for you.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>A credit card is a tool. What is that tool for? The bank divides credit card users into 2&nbsp;categories. There are Debtors and Transactors.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><h4>Debtors</h4><div>A debtor is a person who looks at their checking account and sees $500. Then this Debtor looks at their credit card and sees $1,000 available credit. In their lightning fast mind they see $500+$1,000=$1,500. Whoo Hoo! I can spend $1,500 right now! Momma needs a new pair of shoes... Daddy needs that new sports thingy he's been dreaming about... etc... This person can't pay off that card, so they now set in to the LONG task of making payments on that card. Hopefully they're smart enough to realize they must pay A LOT more than the minimum payment if they ever want to see that card paid off, and more than likely it will take them a year or two to pay it off... unfortunately the mindset that had them creating the debt, usually prevents them from seeing clearly how to get out of debt also.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><h4>Transactors</h4><div><br /></div><div>A Transactor sees that SAME $500 balance in checking and the SAME $1,000 available credit, but does some different math. They say I have $1,000 on the card and $500 in checking $1,000-$500,... So I have $500 to spend this month. In other words this person doesn't see this as extra money. This person sees that they have $500 and they could either spend that with their credit card, debit card, checks, cash, etc... but they still only have $500 to spend.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>A transactor pays off their card, IN FULL EVERY MONTH. They carry no balance over, and pay no interest. They use the card as a tool.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div><h4 style="text-align: center;"><b>Why use a credit card at all? Here are some reasons:</b></h4></div><div><ul><li><b>Protection</b></li><ul><li><b>FRAUD</b>: Your card is lost, someone uses it, you file a claim and the money is refunded. This takes 7-10 business days many times. It doesn't matter if the fraud was on a Credit Card, Debit Card, or Check... It's going to take a little while to refund. BUT on a credit card it's only tying up your "Available Credit" NOT your "Cash Balance" like fraud on a check or debit card would. Yes you'd get the money back either way, but the credit card makes that wait more comfortable.&nbsp;</li><li><b>Purchase Protections</b>: There are things SOME credit cards offer that debit cards typically don't.&nbsp;</li><ul><li>If you rent a car, the credit card may cover the insurance so you don't have to pay extra for that.&nbsp;</li><li>If you pay your monthly cell phone bill through the credit card the card may cover the insurance on the phone so you don't have to pay for that.&nbsp;</li><li>Certain items you purchase are guaranteed against loss or damage.&nbsp;</li></ul></ul><li><b>Rewards</b></li><ul><li><b>GET PAID</b> to spend your own money. With credit card rewards you can earn cash back, gift cards, and more for making purchases you were going to make anyway. This way your money is working for you more.</li><ul><li>Debit Cards used to offer this, and at most banks they don't anymore. But even when they did the rewards were less than the credit cards rewards were.&nbsp;</li></ul></ul><li><b>Building Credit</b></li><ul><li>You CANNOT build a GREAT credit score without a credit card. You can't. There are idiot bloggers out there saying "You don't need a credit card, just do XYZ..." Here's the thing. You can build SOME credit without a credit card.&nbsp;</li><li>You CAN build GOOD/DECENT credit without a credit card. You cannot build GREAT credit without a credit card... more on this in a moment.&nbsp;</li><li>Again, despite the bloggers who love to misinform you, paying rent, utilities, insurance, cell phone bills, etc... does NOTHING for your credit. All of that positive info you are building helps with certain public profiles, but it does not help your credit reports or scores at all.&nbsp;</li><ul><li>NOT paying them may send you to collections which could ruin your credit, but the positive stuff doesn't help. You must pay on a "Credit Product" or "Trade Line" to build credit. These are Credit Cards, Loans, and Lines of Credit. That's pretty much it. Only these build credit.&nbsp;</li></ul></ul></ul></div><h4 style="text-align: center;">The Things You Didn't Think About...</h4><h4 style="text-align: center;">What do you mean by building GREAT credit?&nbsp;</h4><div><br /></div><div>Read more here:&nbsp;<a href="http://www.myfico.com/CreditEducation/WhatsInYourScore.aspx">http://www.myfico.com/CreditEducation/WhatsInYourScore.aspx</a></div><div><br /></div><div>FICO is the company that creates the credit score that most banks use to make credit decisions. They are THE credit score. There are others, but non as popular as FICO. At this time (10/08/2012) FICO doesn't look at your job, income, rent, phone bills, etc. They only look at Credit Products, because they only look at your "Credit Reports" which are reports about how you use your "Credit". There are three credit reporting agencies. <a href="http://www.experian.com/credit-report-partner/index-g.html?WT.srch=ECDG1&amp;bcd=vQwpOf3p&amp;pcrid=14080750749&amp;pmt=e&amp;pkw=experian" target="_blank">Experian</a>, <a href="http://www.transunion.com/personal-credit/marketing/truecredit.page?channel=paid&amp;cid=ppc:google:transunion&amp;gclid=CJHyhdzW8LICFcKPPAodC1QAqg" target="_blank">TransUnion</a>, and <a href="http://www.equifax.com/advantage495/PSbr/?CID=2&amp;equifax_G_E&amp;gclid=CI_669_W8LICFU6mPAodNiQA_A" target="_blank">Equifax</a>.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>You can and SHOULD obtain a free credit report at least once a year, from EACH of the three credit reporting agencies. Each agency will have a different mix of items reported and therefore you have THREE credit scores by default. Go to<a href="https://www.annualcreditreport.com/cra/index.jsp" target="_blank"> www.annualcreditreport.com</a>, or use a free/low cost trail offer of Identity Theft Protection. I prefer the latter because those companies usually tailor the reports to make them easier to read and understand. I also suggest having an on going service with one of these companies because you can keep tabs on your reports that way without affecting your score. Pulling your own score, through any monitors service has NO downside effect on your score.&nbsp;&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Here are some reputable credit report/ID Theft Monitoring companies.</div><div><br /></div><div><ul><li><a href="https://www.wellsfargo.com/insurance/id_credit_protection/idtheft/?ref=WELLSROSPAG0066&amp;eitp=WELLSCONFRM0008" target="_blank">Wells Fargo Identity Theft Protection</a></li><ul><li>I've used this one and like it!</li></ul><li><a href="https://secure.lifelock.com/enrollment/?promocode=next&amp;uid=280001__650646760" target="_blank">LifeLock</a></li><ul><li>Have not used it myself, but heard great things about it from others.</li></ul><li><a href="http://www.identityguard.com/ipages/le4/letp30daysfree1.html?mktp=Next&amp;utm_medium=affiliates&amp;hid=209900856&amp;campid=13&amp;utm_campaign=650646759&amp;c1=280001&amp;c2=650646759&amp;c3=30__id4+830765795____&amp;cenhp1=1" target="_blank">IdentityGaurd</a></li><ul><li>Don't know them but they are among the top listed providers</li></ul><li><a href="https://www.trustedid.com/cmalp9.php?promoRefCode=NEXTWEBDFTTEN&amp;hitid=160455401&amp;hitsid=115" target="_blank">Trusted ID</a></li><ul><li>Don't know them but they are among the top listed providers</li></ul></ul></div><div><br /></div><h4 style="text-align: center;">FICO sees your credit score like this:</h4><div><br /></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/ce-scorebreakdown-250a8133e425.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" height="285" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/ce-scorebreakdown-ffc8d371f062.png" width="640" /></a></div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div><ul><li>Payment History (Making payments on credit products)</li><li>Amounts Owed on credit products</li><li>Length of Credit History ( how long credit products have been open)</li><li>New Credit (when was the last time you applied for new credit)(this is where those "Inquiries" or "Hard Hits" affect you.&nbsp;</li><li>Types of Credit used (They want to see experience with ALL credit types, including credit cards)</li></ul><h4 style="text-align: center;">How does a credit card affect you?</h4></div><div><h4><ul><li>Payment History</li></ul></h4><ol><ul><li>ANY time a credit product shows that something was due, and you paid at least what was due you get an "OK" stamp for that month. Whether you paid a $25 payment on a $300 card balance, or you had a balance of $1.50 and you paid the whole $1.50, either way you get an "OK" stamp for that month.&nbsp;</li><li>Therefore the idea of getting a balance on a credit card and making payments could have some&nbsp;merit... BUT...</li></ul></ol><h4><ul><li>"Usage" part of "Amounts Owed"&nbsp;</li></ul></h4><ol><ul><li>Usage kills the idea of putting a $300 balance on a $300 limit card and making payments on it.&nbsp;</li><li>Usage asks two questions. How much is available on the card? How much are they using?&nbsp;</li><ul><li>$300 credit limit (Available Credit)</li><li>$300 balance on the card</li><li>=100% Usage</li><li>Grade F.</li></ul><li><b>FICO actually wants you to use LESS than 30% of your credit limits, less than 10% would be best.&nbsp;</b></li><ul><li>$300 credit limit</li><li>$1.50 Balance</li><li>= 0.5% Usage</li><li>Grade A</li></ul><li>To figure out how much of your credit limit you can use and stay in good graces take the credit limit ($300) times 10% (0.10) [or 30% max (0.30)]. On a $300 credit limit you should not use more than $30-$90 during the whole month.&nbsp;</li><ul><li>$300 credit limit</li><li>$30 balance ($90 balance)</li><li>=10% Usage (30% Usage)</li><li>Grade A</li></ul><li><b><u>If you pay the balance in full you will not pay interest.</u> &nbsp;But the card, and credit report, will STILL show your balance as of the statement date so don't use more than 30% tops!</b></li></ul></ol><h4><ul><li>Length of Credit History</li></ul></h4><ul><ul><li><b>The older your credit history and average age of all your credit accounts, the better.&nbsp;</b></li><li><b>Credit Cards (and some other credit lines) are the only products <u>that stay open forever</u>.</b></li><li>Loans (Mortgages, Student Loans, Auto Loans, Etc) all eventually get paid off, refinanced, or closed/sold). Every time you close a loan it is removed from the average age of your open accounts. Every time you get a new loan it reduces the average age of your accounts.&nbsp;</li><li>If you have 3 CC's. One is open 2 years and two are open 5 years. Your average age is 4 years. (2+5+5)/3=4.</li><li>Close one of the 5 year old cards (2+5)/2=3.5 and you now have an average age of 3.5 years.</li><li>Close the 2 year old card (5+5)/2= 5 and you now have an average age of 5 years.</li><li>FICO prefers that you have 1-3 credit cards and no more. Department store cards like dillards, kohls, target and walmart count as credit cards. You should have no more than 3. Closing them may hurt you because of the above math.&nbsp;</li><li>Because you don't want to open new cards or close old ones unless you have to, choose 2 great cards, keep them active, used regularly, and open forever. Never close a card unless you must or get a new one unless there is a significantly good reason to do so. If you already have 5 credit cards do more research before closing any.&nbsp;</li></ul></ul><h4><ul><li>TYPES of credit used</li></ul></h4><ul><ul><li>The more variety of good history you have the more secure your credit looks to be.&nbsp;</li><li>Some people with high credit scores are still declined for auto loans because they've had no "loan" experience. Adding a credit card or two can balance out your experience.&nbsp;</li></ul></ul><div><br /></div></div><div><br /></div><h4 style="text-align: center;">BOTTOM LINE, My Advice:</h4><br /><b>Get 1 or 2 credit cards (NOT 3). Make sure these are cards you can use everywhere (Visa, Master, Discover, Amex logos) NOT a department store card or jewelry store card. These should be cards you can use frequently for small amounts. Walmart/Target may be OK, IF you shop at walmart/target <u>frequently already</u>.&nbsp;</b><br /><b><br /></b><b>Go out ONCE A MONTH and make a RIDICULOUSLY SMALL purchase. Go buy a $1.50 Dr Pepper, a $5 Cheeseburger, or $30 in gas. Put the card in the back of your wallet and don't touch it again. Wait for your statement, PAY IT IN FULL!</b><br /><b><br /></b>This way you'll NEVER pay interest on a credit card, you'll NEVER get into credit card debt, and you'll STILL build credit. In fact, because of usage scoring, your credit will be BETTER than the one who put the $300 balance on his card and made min payments for a year.<br /><br />Do this every month, from here on and into the future. DO NOT put big purchases on your card. DO NOT use more than 30% of your&nbsp;available&nbsp;credit during the course of the month. DO NOT use more than 2 cards (3 if you must, no more). DO NOT EVER carry a balance over (paying less than the total statement balance).<br /><br />It's not that hard. If you don't have the discipline to make one purchase and pay it off you shouldn't be even talking about credit. You should be building a&nbsp;habit&nbsp;of saving and budgeting first. Until you have a savings started, it's a waste of time to start building your credit. Once you have developed the habit of saving and NOT touching that savings, and you are working some form of a budget, THEN build credit. Don't touch credit until you have that down first.<br /><div><b><br /></b></div><div><b>Live Long and Prosper! Selah... Darrell G. Wolfe</b></div><div><br /></div><div>If you need to get out of debt and become wealthy. Check out this AMAZING step by step guide. It's much more simple than you think!</div><div><b><br /></b></div><div><table cellspacing="0" id="detailheader" style="color: black; font-family: 'Times New Roman', serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; text-align: start; width: 482px;"><tbody><tr><td id="detailImage" style="padding: 5px 20px 5px 5px; vertical-align: top;"><div class="relative" style="position: relative;"><a href="http://astore.amazon.com/your0f4d-20/images/159555078X" id="imageViewerLink" style="color: #181ecd;" target="ImageView"><img alt="The Total Money Makeover: A Proven Plan for Financial Fitness" id="detailProductImage" src="/images/imported/ecx-images-amazon-com/517lv72u4vl-sl210-980affe66cdb.jpg" style="border: none; max-width: 300px;" /></a></div></td><td style="vertical-align: top;"><div id="titleAndByLine" style="margin-bottom: 5px;"><h2 style="font-size: 16px; margin: 0px; padding: 0px;">The Total Money Makeover: A Proven Plan for Financial Fitness<br /><span class="by" style="font-size: 9pt; font-weight: normal;">By Dave Ramsey</span></h2></div><table id="prices" style="font-size: 9pt; margin: 0px;"><tbody><tr id="listprice"><td class="pricelabel" style="font-size: 10.5pt; font-weight: bold; text-align: right; vertical-align: bottom; white-space: nowrap;">List Price:</td><td style="vertical-align: bottom;"><span class="amount" id="detailListPrice" style="font-size: 10.5pt; text-decoration: line-through;">$24.99</span></td></tr><tr id="ourprice"><td class="pricelabel" style="font-size: 10.5pt; font-weight: bold; text-align: right; vertical-align: top; white-space: nowrap;">Price:</td><td style="vertical-align: top;"><span class="amount" id="detailOfferPrice" style="font-size: 10.5pt; font-weight: bold;">$14.66</span><span class="supersaver">&nbsp;&amp; eligible for FREE Super Saver Shipping on orders over $25.&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?tag=your0f4d-20&amp;linkCode=sb1&amp;camp=212353&amp;creative=380561&amp;pop-up=1&amp;nodeId=527692" style="color: #181ecd;">Details</a></td></tr></tbody></table><form action="http://astore.amazon.com/your0f4d-20/cart/add/159555078X" id="addToCartForm" method="post" name="buybox" style="margin: 10px 0px 0px;"><input alt="Add to cart" border="0" height="27" id="buybutton" name="pngImage" src="/images/imported/g-ecx-images-amazon-com/add-to-cart-yellow-v156424126-6a3277ce7d36.png" type="image" width="159" /></form><br /><b>Availability:&nbsp;</b>Usually ships in 24 hours<br />Ships from and sold by Amazon.com<br /><a href="http://www.amazon.com/gp/offer-listing/159555078X?tag=your0f4d-20&amp;linkCode=sb1&amp;camp=212353&amp;creative=380553" style="color: #181ecd;" target="_blank">226 new or used available from $9.23</a><br />Average customer review:&nbsp;<a href="http://www.amazon.com/product-reviews/159555078X?tag=your0f4d-20&amp;linkCode=sb1&amp;camp=212353&amp;creative=380553" style="color: #181ecd;" target="_blank"><img height="11" name="pngImage" src="/images/imported/images-amazon-com/star45-tpng-ea65fd3a5f60.png" style="border: none;" width="56" /></a><br />(<a href="http://www.amazon.com/product-reviews/159555078X?tag=your0f4d-20&amp;linkCode=sb1&amp;camp=212353&amp;creative=380553" style="color: #181ecd;" target="_blank">549 customer reviews</a>)</td></tr></tbody></table></div><div><a href="https://www.mytotalmoneymakeover.com/">https://www.mytotalmoneymakeover.com/</a><br /><br /></div><div><b>Have questions about anything I said here? Disagree? Have an update? Want to share your story? Comment Below!</b></div><div><br /></div><div><b><br class="Apple-interchange-newline" />I call you empowered 2 prosper with good success!</b><br /><b><br /></b><b>Darrell G. Wolfe</b><br /><a href="http://n2gs.blogspot.com/">http://n2gs.blogspot.com/</a><br /><a href="https://www.facebook.com/n2goodsuccess?sk=wall">https://www.facebook.com/n2goodsuccess</a><br /><br /><b style="background-color: white;">See Also:</b><br />Book Suggestion from the&nbsp;<a href="http://astore.amazon.com/your0f4d-20/190-6424964-9305846" target="_blank">N2 Good Success Amazon Store</a><br />Product Offerings from&nbsp;<a href="http://www.zazzle.com/n2goodsuccess" target="_blank">N2 Good Success Zazzle Store</a><br /><br /><b>Bulldog Publications:&nbsp;</b><br /><b><a href="http://www.amazon.com/dp/1453815619/ref=as_li_ss_til?tag=your0f4d-20&amp;camp=213381&amp;creative=390973&amp;linkCode=as4&amp;creativeASIN=1453815619&amp;adid=1NAWEWPYWDQA3XGCY7N5&amp;&amp;ref-refURL=http%3A%2F%2Fn2gs.blogspot.com%2F" target="_blank">Bankings Basics for Believers; Now Available on Amazon.&nbsp;</a></b><br />Print&nbsp;Edition<br /><iframe frameborder="0" marginheight="0" marginwidth="0" scrolling="no" src="http://rcm.amazon.com/e/cm?lt1=_blank&amp;bc1=000000&amp;IS2=1&amp;bg1=FFFFFF&amp;fc1=000000&amp;lc1=0000FF&amp;t=your0f4d-20&amp;o=1&amp;p=8&amp;l=as4&amp;m=amazon&amp;f=ifr&amp;ref=ss_til&amp;asins=1453815619" style="height: 240px; width: 120px;"></iframe><br /><br />Kindle Edition<br /><iframe frameborder="0" marginheight="0" marginwidth="0" scrolling="no" src="http://rcm.amazon.com/e/cm?lt1=_blank&amp;bc1=000000&amp;IS2=1&amp;bg1=FFFFFF&amp;fc1=000000&amp;lc1=0000FF&amp;t=your0f4d-20&amp;o=1&amp;p=8&amp;l=as4&amp;m=amazon&amp;f=ifr&amp;ref=ss_til&amp;asins=B008EO488Y" style="height: 240px; width: 120px;"></iframe><br /><br /><iframe frameborder="0" marginheight="0" marginwidth="0" scrolling="no" src="http://rcm.amazon.com/e/cm?lt1=_blank&amp;bc1=000000&amp;IS2=1&amp;bg1=FFFFFF&amp;fc1=000000&amp;lc1=0000FF&amp;t=your0f4d-20&amp;o=1&amp;p=8&amp;l=as4&amp;m=amazon&amp;f=ifr&amp;ref=ss_til&amp;asins=0785263268" style="height: 240px; width: 120px;"></iframe> </div>
